control_objective: Replace long-lived cloud keys with GitHub Actions OIDC federation and short-lived token exchange.
implementation: GitHub Actions OIDC federation validates access to Yandex Cloud without static cloud keys.
portfolio_claim: OIDC-based CI/CD authentication was validated without long-lived cloud key material.
framework_mapping: NIST SSDF PW.6; SLSA Build L2/L3 pattern; CIS IAM least privilege principle

control_objective: Use scoped service accounts for bootstrap, registry, audit, and short-lived Managed Kubernetes workloads.
implementation: Bootstrap service accounts were retained separately from temporary MKS runtime service accounts; temporary MKS identities were destroyed after cloud evidence runs.
portfolio_claim: Temporary runtime identities were separated from retained bootstrap identities and removed after validation.
framework_mapping: CIS IAM least privilege; NIST CSF 2.0 PR.AA

control_objective: Enable audit trail collection and preserve sanitized public evidence for cloud actions.
implementation: Audit Trails resource was retained and validated as part of the bootstrap evidence track.
portfolio_claim: Cloud audit evidence was integrated into the evidence register and gap register.
framework_mapping: NIST CSF 2.0 DE.CM; ISO 27001 logging/audit control pattern

control_objective: Provision and destroy cloud infrastructure through controlled Terraform runs.
implementation: Managed Kubernetes, network, service accounts, node group, and cleanup paths were executed with Terraform and verified after destroy.
portfolio_claim: Cloud resources were created and destroyed through IaC with post-destroy verification.
framework_mapping: NIST SSDF PO.5; CIS IaC governance pattern

control_objective: Demonstrate real Managed Kubernetes lifecycle evidence in Yandex Cloud.
implementation: Short-lived MKS clusters were created for baseline/remediation and Kyverno validation, then destroyed.
portfolio_claim: Managed Kubernetes was validated using real cloud infrastructure, not only local manifests.

control_objective: Move from insecure workload patterns to hardened runtime controls.
implementation: Hardened workload requirements include non-root execution, privilege escalation denial, read-only root filesystem, dropped capabilities, RuntimeDefault seccomp, and registry-controlled image source.
portfolio_claim: Hardened workload controls were validated through server-side admission.
framework_mapping: CIS Kubernetes Pod Security; NSA/CISA Kubernetes Hardening pattern

control_objective: Enforce workload security requirements at admission time.
implementation: Kyverno admission policy denied the insecure workload and allowed the hardened workload.
portfolio_claim: Kyverno admission policy enforcement was proven on Yandex Managed Kubernetes.
framework_mapping: CIS Kubernetes Admission Control; NIST SSDF PW.8

control_objective: Build and push container images through OIDC-authenticated CI/CD into Yandex Container Registry.
implementation: GitHub Actions exchanged an OIDC assertion for a Yandex Cloud IAM token and pushed controlled demo images to YCR.
portfolio_claim: CI/CD image publishing was validated through OIDC and Yandex Container Registry.
framework_mapping: SLSA build provenance pattern; NIST SSDF PS.3

control_objective: Generate SBOM evidence for insecure and hardened container images.
implementation: Syft generated CycloneDX SBOMs for registry-pushed insecure and hardened images.
portfolio_claim: SBOM evidence exists for both vulnerable baseline and hardened image.
framework_mapping: NIST SSDF PS.3; SLSA dependency visibility

control_objective: Measure vulnerability reduction between insecure and hardened images.
implementation: Trivy and Grype scans were executed against registry-pushed images, and final remediation metrics were consolidated.
portfolio_claim: The hardened image showed measurable vulnerability reduction compared with the insecure baseline.
framework_mapping: NIST SSDF RV.1; NIST CSF 2.0 ID.RA

control_objective: Keep public evidence sanitized and prevent accidental publication of secrets, kubeconfigs, or Terraform state.
implementation: Evidence files were sanitized and repository safety checks used gitleaks plus explicit artifact guards.
portfolio_claim: Evidence publication controls were implemented and validated before commits.
framework_mapping: NIST CSF 2.0 PR.DS; Secure evidence handling pattern

control_objective: Avoid uncontrolled paid cloud resource retention after validation runs.
implementation: Temporary MKS resources were destroyed after evidence collection; bootstrap resources remain intentionally retained until final cleanup decision.
portfolio_claim: Temporary paid MKS resources were removed and verified after the cloud evidence run.

control_objective: Keep public claims aligned with validated evidence and avoid overstating unproven capabilities.
implementation: Cloud evidence gap register, evidence index, and final remediation metrics constrain the final portfolio narrative.
portfolio_claim: Validated claims are tied to committed evidence paths.
